svg.text: LAO (ພາສາລາວ) / ZHUANG (壯語 · VAHCUENGH)
TAI-KADAI FAMILY · HISTORY & SHARED HERITAGE
PLATE 41D ALT · HISTORY & CULTURAL LEGACY
— LAO: HISTORY & LITERARY TRADITION —
LAN XANG KINGDOM (~1353–1707 CE)
"Land of a Million Elephants" — unified Lao-speaking peoples of the Mekong valley
Script and Theravada Buddhism arrived together; temples became literacy centers
Classical literature: Phra Lak Phra Lam (Lao Ramayana), Sin Xay epic poem
COLONIAL PERIOD & MODERN NATION
French Indochina 1893–1953 · Lao language suppressed in favor of French
Independence 1953 · Pathet Lao 1975 → Lao PDR (People's Democratic Republic)
Lao declared sole official language; Lao script standardized under socialist policy
ISAN: THE INVISIBLE 23 MILLION
Isan dialect (NE Thailand): ~23M speakers of a Lao variety, not called "Lao"
Written in Thai script · politically Thai · linguistically Lao
More native Lao-speakers live in Thailand than in Laos itself
LAO TRADITIONAL MUSIC & ORAL POETRY
Mor lam: traditional sung poetry form; mɔː = expert, lam = to sing
Competitions feature improvised verse in call-and-response format
Instrument: khaen (bamboo mouth organ) — UNESCO intangible heritage (2017)
SURVIVAL PHRASE
ຂ້ອຍ ຮຽນ ພາສາ ລາວ · /kʰɔːj hian pʰaːsaː laːw/
"I am studying the Lao language"
ຍິນດີທີ່ໄດ້ຮູ້ຈັກ · Nice to meet you (lit. "glad to have met")
ZHUANG (VAHCUENGH) — HISTORY & CULTURAL LEGACY
— ZHUANG: HISTORY & IDENTITY —
INDIGENOUS PRESENCE: 5,000+ YEARS IN GUANGXI
Zhuang are the largest ethnic minority in China (~17M) · Guangxi home for millennia
Ancient Luoyue/Baiyue peoples identified as Zhuang ancestors
Bronze drum tradition (~700 BCE): still used in ritual; UNESCO intangible heritage
SAWNDIP: SCRIPT AS CULTURAL MEMORY
Sawndip (~方块壮字) developed ~10th c. CE · over 10,000 characters documented
Used for Zhuang folk songs (baeuqrox), rituals, and epics — not official literacy
Each village may use different character forms — no central standardization
GUANGXI ZHUANG AUTONOMOUS REGION (1958)
Established 1958 under PRC minority-nationality policy
Zhuang Latin alphabet created 1957 for mass literacy — revised 1982
Today: Mandarin dominant in education; Zhuang taught in some primary schools
FOLK SONG TRADITION: LIUGONG MOUNTAIN FESTIVAL
Third day of third lunar month: Liu Sanjie ("Third Sister Liu") festival
Antiphonal singing (haj) between villages: a living oral literature tradition
Liu Sanjie legend: peasant woman who sang truth to power, became immortal
THE TAI-KADAI CONTINUUM
Zhuang · Lao · Thai · Shan · Dai · Tay-Nung · Bouyei — all Tai-Kadai
Scattered across S. China, Laos, Thailand, Vietnam, NE India — 100M+ speakers
SURVIVAL PHRASE (ZHUANG LATIN)
Gou roxnyinh Vahcuengh · "I understand (know) the Zhuang language"

THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
